extra.reasoning: The property GLOBALLY(opted_out implies not sent) holds. The only way to set sent=true after opt-out is t_resume_glitch, which requires opted_out AND acc==9. I checked whether acc can reach 9. Each step event applies a modular linear recurrence acc := (3*acc + c) mod 48 with c=24 (step_0), c=28 (step_1), c=26 (step_2). I ran a BFS over acc values starting from acc=0 and enumerated the full reachable set: exactly the 24 even values {0,2,4,...,46}. The target 9 is odd and never appears. This follows from a parity invariant: acc starts at 0 (even); 3*even is even; adding any of the even constants 24/28/26 keeps it even; and reduction mod 48 (an even modulus) preserves parity. Hence acc is always even and acc==9 is unreachable, so t_resume_glitch can never fire. After opt-out, send_attempt only matches t_send_blocked (opted_out and acc != 9), which sets sent:=false. opted_out is only ever set together with sent:=false, and no enabled transition sets sent=true while opted_out is true. Therefore no event sequence (within the 50-event bound, and in fact ever) violates the property. The target 9 is unreachable; no counterexample exists.
